methods.description: Validation of a developed analytical method for detecting NSAIDs in water samples using Magnetic Solid Phase Extraction (MSPE) with MSp-TDI-βCD. The validation process evaluates key analytical parameters including linearity, limit of detection (LOD), limit of quantification (LOQ), precision (intra-day and inter-day), and recovery.

steps.description: Evaluate linearity to validate the developed method. All tested NSAIDs showed good linearity with good coefficiency of determination (R 2 ≥ 0.990).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure analytical response for standard solutions at different concentrations to assess linearity and calculate the coefficient of determination (R^2).
inputs: Standard solutions of analytes (NSAIDs) at various concentrations

steps.description: The LOD was calculated based on signal-to-noise ratio (3 SD/m) (n = 10) and the values obtained were in the range 0.16-0.37 ng ml −1 .
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the limit of detection (LOD) using the 3*SD/slope method, based on the standard deviation of blank measurements or low-concentration standards (SD) and the slope of the calibration curve (m), using n=10 measurements.
inputs: Standard deviation of blank measurements or low-concentration standards (SD)

steps.description: The LOQ (10 × SD/m) (n = 10) was also calculated and the values obtained were in the range 0.53-1.22 ng ml −1 .
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the limit of quantification (LOQ) using the 10*SD/slope method, based on the standard deviation of blank measurements or low-concentration standards (SD) and the slope of the calibration curve (m), using n=10 measurements.
inputs: Standard deviation of blank measurements or low-concentration standards (SD)

steps.description: Precision was studied in terms of repeatability (intra-day) (n = 5) expressed as relative standard deviation (RSD). The inter-day precision was demonstrated by performing five replicates of standard solutions on a single day.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure analytical response for replicate standard solutions within a single day (n=5) to assess intra-day precision (repeatability) and calculate the relative standard deviation (RSD).
inputs: Standard solutions at a specific concentration
outputs: Relative standard deviation (RSD) for intra-day precision

steps.description: The inter-day precision of the MSPE procedure was performed by performing standard solutions of the same concentration levels in five replicates on each of the three consecutive days (n=15 total). Expressed as relative standard deviation (RSD).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure analytical response for replicate standard solutions across multiple days (n=5 replicates per day for 3 days, total n=15) to assess inter-day precision (reproducibility) and calculate the relative standard deviation (RSD).
inputs: Standard solutions at a specific concentration
Analytical instrument measurements (n=5 replicates per day for 3 days)
outputs: Relative standard deviation (RSD) for inter-day precision

steps.description: Percentage relative recovery and RSD (n = 5) for spiked NSAIDs from different water samples using MSp-TDI-βCD for the MSPE method.
steps.searchable_summary: Spike known amounts of analytes into different sample matrices (e.g., tap, drinking, river water), perform the analytical method (MSPE), measure the recovered amounts (n=5 replicates per matrix), and calculate the percentage recovery and relative standard deviation (RSD).
inputs: Different water samples (tap water, drinking water, river water)
Spiked analytes (NSAIDs) at known concentration levels
Analytical instrument measurements (n=5 replicates per matrix)
